title: Helmet Forge: Repair & Equip Arena
description: Michelle stands in a forge arena surrounded by 5 floating DamagedHelmet fragments arranged in a circle. The player must collect energy orbs, channel them into helmet fragments in the correct sequence to repair the helmet, equip it onto Michelle, and then undo/redo repair steps to achieve the optimal enchantment combination before confirming the final build.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements a helmet forge mini-game. Load Michelle.glb at center (scale 1.5) with idle animation playing. Load DamagedHelmet.glb and create 5 cloned instances arranged in a circle (radius 3) around Michelle at height 2, each slowly rotating. Add a PerspectiveCamera at (0,3,8) with OrbitControls. Use ambient light (0.4) plus a warm point light (orange, intensity 2) below the helmets simulating forge glow, and a directional light from above. Implement these interactions: (1) Click the ground plane to spawn energy orbs (glowing spheres with emissive material) up to a max of 5 total, tracking an energy counter displayed in an HTML overlay. (2) Drag an energy orb onto a helmet fragment to charge it — the fragment glows brighter and scales up 10%. Each fragment needs exactly 1 orb. Conservation law: total orbs spawned minus orbs consumed always equals orbs visible on field. (3) Charged fragments must be activated in clockwise order by clicking them — clicking out of order resets that fragment (orb is refunded). (4) Once all 5 fragments are activated in order, they animate (lerp) toward Michelle's head position and merge into a single helmet placed on her head. Michelle plays a celebration animation or pose shift. (5) Implement full undo/redo with Z/Y keys: undo reverses the last action (orb spawn, orb assignment, fragment activation) restoring previous state exactly. Redo re-applies. Maintain a history stack of at least 12 states. (6) After equipping, a panel shows 3 enchantment toggle buttons (Fire/Ice/Lightning) that change the helmet emissive color. Player can undo enchantment choices too. (7) A Confirm button locks the final build and displays a score based on steps taken. Show step counter and energy counter in a top-left HUD. Use raycasting for all click/drag interactions. Add a subtle particle system around charged fragments using Points geometry.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  energyOrbsSpawned: number,         // total energy orbs created by clicking ground
  energyOrbsConsumed: number,         // orbs used to charge helmet fragments
  energyOrbsVisible: number,         // orbs currently on field (spawned - consumed)
  fragmentsCharged: number,         // helmet fragments that have received an orb
  fragmentsActivated: number,         // fragments activated in correct clockwise sequence
  helmetEquipped: boolean,         // whether assembled helmet is on Michelle's head
  repairHistoryLength: number,         // count of repair steps in undo/redo stack
  currentHistoryIndex: number,         // current position in repair history (for undo/redo)
  michelleAnimationPlaying: boolean,         // whether Michelle's idle animation is active
  forgeGlowIntensity: number,         // intensity of the forge point light below helmets
  optimalSequenceAchieved: boolean,         // whether player achieved optimal repair path
}
